mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-run trend rate compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and increases in the consumer price index indicate stagflationary supply shocks that erode real corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when stagflation occurs, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases above 0.50 percent during a tightening cycle raise discount rates and compress equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 60% of the duration during identified tightening cycles, or the forward return differential between tightening and easing periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which supports near-term retail and construction earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 in a single month confirms robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS exceeds 200,000, or the signal's predictive accuracy degrades to chance levels after the first 12 months of out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and industrial production confirms synchronized global demand recovery, which lifts cyclical equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns when both series expand, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising short-term rates combined with falling revolving consumer credit indicate monetary tightening that restricts household liquidity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises and RSAFS falls in the same month
failure_condition: Equity markets rise for more than 60% of instances where rates rise and credit falls, or the joint signal shows no incremental explanatory power over FEDFUNDS alone in a nested F-test.
